description: Personal Next.js golden tasks for FastContext standalone local exploration on the Ernaering app.

tasks.task: Find the BMI calculator submit flow that computes BMI, Mifflin BMR, TDEE, method comparison, protein needs, and optional nutrition risk.

expected_citations.reason: BmiForm owns input state and handleSubmit computes BMI, energy estimates, protein requirements, and optional nutrition risk.
Pure BMI, Mifflin, Henry/Oxford, and NASEM/DRI energy formulas live here.

tasks.task: Find where protein requirement profiles and grams or energy-percent calculations are implemented.

expected_citations.reason: Protein requirement profiles and calculateProteinRequirements live here.
The calculator attaches proteinRequirements to the displayed result here.

tasks.task: Find the optional nutrition-risk screening logic based on previous weight, date, BMI, and age.

expected_citations.reason: classifyNutritionRisk implements weight-loss, BMI, and age criteria.
The calculator invokes nutrition-risk classification when optional previous-weight fields are set.

tasks.task: Find the main intake registration UI that wires day state, meal tabs, summaries, and the active meal section.

expected_citations.reason: The /inntak route renders the intake form.
InntakForm wires intake state, totals, summary scope, and layout-level state.
The same form renders meal tabs, active MealSection, export panel, and daily summary.
The state hook wires active day, active meal, copy selectors, and current meal state used by the shell.

tasks.task: Find where intake state handles active day and meal selection, item updates, copy day or meal, and localStorage lifecycle.

expected_citations.reason: The hook owns days, active day, active meal, copy selectors, and transient UI state.
The hook implements add/update/remove/move items plus copy and clear operations.
Storage lifecycle reads, migrates, writes, and clears local intake state.

tasks.task: Find the parse-food API route that validates request size/body, calls OpenAI parsing, caps parsed items, matches foods, and returns trace data.

expected_citations.reason: The POST route validates input, invokes parseFoodText, matches parsed mentions, and builds response trace.
parseFoodText sends the structured OpenAI request and reads output text.

tasks.task: Find where the OpenAI food parser prompt/schema and runtime normalization reject malformed parsed food items.

expected_citations.reason: Parser instructions and JSON schema constrain the model output shape.
normalizeParsedMentions validates, trims, rejects malformed items, and normalizes query fields.

tasks.task: Find the deterministic food matching pipeline that combines lexical, alias, semantic, modifier, portion, and decision signals.

expected_citations.reason: The matcher orchestrates lexical/alias/semantic candidates, quantity, alternatives, decision, and trace fields.
Decision thresholds choose auto_select, needs_review, or manual_required.
Hybrid candidate finalization, decision invocation, risk reason fallback, quantity, and match-signal helpers live here.

tasks.task: Find the optional semantic food search support that embeds normalized food queries and reads Supabase pgVector matches.

expected_citations.reason: Semantic matching embeds the query, calls the Supabase RPC, parses rows, and maps them back to food candidates.
The matcher decides when semantic support is used and merges semantic candidate scores.

tasks.task: Find the manual Matvaretabellen search path used by the intake UI for direct food lookup.

expected_citations.reason: The GET /api/food-search route validates query length and calls searchFoodsForUi.
The browser helper calls /api/food-search and validates the response.
MealSection performs manual search and creates manual food items from selected results.

tasks.task: Find where daily and average nutrition summaries render macro cards, micro nutrient rows, safety warnings, and summary calculations.

expected_citations.reason: DailySummary renders day/average scope, micro groups, safety warnings, and DailyTotals.
DailyTotals renders macro cards and breakdown tooltips.
Meal, daily, all-days, and average nutrition totals are calculated here.

tasks.task: Find the intake export and print flow that creates report sessions, opens the print route, and renders summary or comparison views.

expected_citations.reason: The export panel copies food lists and opens the print view after creating a report session.
Report sessions are serialized, parsed, clamped, and normalized here.
The print view reads the report session, controls summary/comparison mode, and handles copy actions.
The static report HTML builder creates summary, food-list, average summary, and comparison report sections.

tasks.task: Find Playwright tests that cover seeded intake storage, manual food search/editing, match-state styling, portion chips, and helper data builders.

expected_citations.reason: Seeded localStorage and copy-day/copy-meal intake flows are tested here.
Manual search, edit panels, match-state styling, portion chips, and scroll behavior are tested here.
Shared Playwright helpers and seeded food builders live here.

tasks.task: Find Playwright tests that verify the calculator desktop layout, mobile stacking, method comparison, protein needs, and clinical source info panel.

expected_citations.reason: Calculator UI tests cover desktop layout, mobile stacking, method comparison, protein rows, and source links.

tasks.task: Find the app shell, route redirects, and navigation links between intake and calculator pages.

expected_citations.reason: Root layout sets metadata, navigation, page container, Analytics, and Speed Insights.
AppNav defines intake/calculator links and active navigation state.
The home route redirects to /inntak.
